files.SKILL.md:
# Phases

Read → Act → Verify → Close. Trim later phases when earlier run long. Never trim Close.

## Read

Read the instruction once. Count the named deliverables — that count defines done. Stop reading when you can act.

Value not available: mark missing, move on. Do not substitute something plausible.

## Act

Rank before starting: active incidents > named-external > named-internal on a deadline > admin acks. Live fire beats messages. Named external beats internal peer.

No second turn on any thread until every reply-worthy thread has had its first. Acks are one-line and cheap; skipping them is how urgent items go cold.

Every outgoing value traces to a read from this task. Can't cite the read, don't send it. Quote verbatim where wording matters.

One record, one named owner, one concrete next move.

Concrete times. No "soon."

Detail narrows as audience widens. Peers: operational. Oversight: status and risk. External: impact and next step. Internal identifiers and sensitive topics stay internal — even naming their existence publicly leaks.

## Verify

Look at the thing you changed, not only the response.

One situation on multiple surfaces — chat, tracker, email for the named person waiting — is one analysis, several landings. Reuse the analysis, never the landing.

## Close

Four numbers: deliverables asked, landed, threads opened, closed-or-parked. Mismatch → keep going or park with a reason.

Record patterns, not specifics.

## Pacing

Read long → cut Act to highest-ranked. Act long → verify only the most consequential. Never cut Close. Stop when satisfied, not when you feel done.
